_meta.description: Legal scaffolding — IP, trademarks, contracts, data processing, privacy, terms. Pairs phosphorus31.org/legal/ public surfaces.
_meta.disclaimer: This is the operator's own organizational policy file, not legal advice. Material changes should be reviewed by counsel.

ownership.principle: Work-for-hire and contributor-license-agreement frame all IP; nonprofit retains copyright on funded artifacts; nothing belongs to the operator personally except pre-existing personal projects clearly delimited in /home/p31 outside the nonprofit corpus.
ownership.founderContributions: Pre-incorporation founder code is being progressively assigned to P31 Labs, Inc. via documented IP-assignment agreement; tracker in legal/ip-assignment-log.md

code.rationale: Copyleft prevents enterprise extraction without contribution back

alternatives.MIT: for utility libraries that benefit from broad adoption
alternatives.CC0: for documentation snippets and configuration examples

designAssets.rationale: Brand mark stays controlled; visual canon shareable for non-commercial use

thirdPartyDependencies.tool: scripts/license-audit.mjs (runs in CI as part of security:check)
thirdPartyDependencies.compatibilityMatrix: AGPL-3.0 + MIT + Apache-2.0 + BSD-3 OK; LGPL OK with care; GPL-2-only excluded; SSPL excluded

trademarks.infringementResponse: Polite cease-and-desist (operator-drafted) before counsel engagement; document everything

review.principle: Operator self-drafts using templates; counsel-of-record (TBD) reviews any contract > $10k OR with novel structure
redLineRules: No indemnification giving away nonprofit status protections
No exclusive licenses to nonprofit's mission-critical IP
No arbitration clauses that prevent class participation
No automatic renewal without 30+ day notice window

principles: We do not track. No third-party analytics, no ad pixels, no tracking cookies.
We do not sell or share donor data.
Operator-only surfaces are local-first (command center, ops console).
Public surfaces collect only what's necessary for the immediate purpose (donation, contact form, passkey).

compliance: Lawful basis: consent for marketing; legitimate interest for transactional
Data subject rights: access, rectification, erasure, portability — all honored within 30 days
Data Processing Agreement with all processors (Stripe, Cloudflare, etc.)
'Do not sell' link present on all donation pages (we don't sell, but link required for transparency)
Verified consumer requests honored within 45 days

HIPAA.applicability: Only if we ever store PHI; currently we do not
HIPAA.ifTriggered: Sign BAA with all processors; implement HIPAA security rule technical safeguards

COPPA.compliance: Currently no surface targets under-13; if youth-vibe-coding scales beyond household, parental consent flow must be added

dataSubjectRequests.responseSLA: 30 days (GDPR) or 45 days (CCPA), whichever applies first
dataSubjectRequests.verification: email confirmation + (for sensitive deletions) one-time code

regulatorsNotified: state AG of any affected resident
EU DPA if EU subjects affected
breachNotificationProtocol.subjectsNotified: all affected within 72 hours; method: email + public notice on /privacy/incidents/

terms.rationale: Pro-operator, pro-litigant terms; we don't trade away rights as a precondition of using free tools

contributorLicenseAgreement.rationale: AGPL itself preserves rights; CLAs add friction without commensurate benefit at our scale
contributorLicenseAgreement.revisitWhen: we have multiple non-employee staff contributors

developerCertificateOfOrigin.enforcement: git commit signed-off-by line; CI checks for it

fincenBeneficialOwnership.applicable: verify nonprofit exemption — most 501(c)(3) orgs are exempt

current.relevanceToNonprofit: operator's pro-se case; informs operator-advocacy program; no nonprofit funds expended on personal litigation
current.disclosureNote: Visible on operator's public record; nonprofit maintains arms-length

disputesAndLitigation.policy: Nonprofit funds may not be expended on operator personal litigation. Educational use of operator's litigation experience (templates, blog posts, advocacy) is permissible and expected — that is mission-aligned.
